data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow continue to rally after softer inflation report
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages rose on Wednesday, a day after the S&P and the Nasdaq closed higher, as soft inflation data soothed sentiments.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.2%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.49%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.02%.

Wall Street's benchmark S&P 500 index and tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite gauge on Tuesday extended a massive rally from the previous session, as sentiment was boosted by soft inflation data. The blue-chip Dow bucked the trend, weighed down by a plunge in component UnitedHealth.

“It's been another 24 hours where the Trump administration continues to hog the headlines. With the president in the Middle East, various stories on AI supported a huge tech-led rally...that got a further boost thanks to a softer-than-expected CPI print,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

On Tuesday, the headline and core consumer prices rose +0.2% M/M, below the +0.3% consensus. On an annual basis, headline CPI came in at +2.3%, also below the +2.4% estimate, while core CPI was in line at +2.8%. 

“Our U.S. economists think the April data is still too early for the Liberation Day tariffs to show up in the aggregate numbers, and they don’t expect the effects to show up in consumer prices until June,” Reid added.

U.S. consumer price inflation did not show many trade tax consequences. The slowing of tourism-related prices might reflect how visitors are wary of entering the U.S., but complications in timing Easter’s effect are also relevant. Appliance price inflation is a concern, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The economic calendar is light today. The wholesale inflation data is expected to land on Thursday.

data.title: BofA Securities: Record Private Clients Buying Streak Since 2008
data.description:
BofA clients bought around $3.2 billion in U.S. equities for a third consecutive week last week, with private clients buying stocks in all sectors as well as ETFs.

Private clients have been buyers for 22 weeks straight, which is the longest buying streak in its data history since ’08. On the other hand, institutional clients were sellers for the first time in three weeks.

“This was the first week of net buying by hedge funds in five weeks (and only the third week of buying YTD) – cumulative sales by hedge funds YTD are the largest of any comparable period in our data since ’08,” said Jill Carey Hall, BofA Securities equity and quant strategist.

CFTC data have similarly suggested net short positions by speculative traders all year, potentially suggesting a pain trade for hedge funds if the rally continues, said Hall in a note.

The Consumer Discretionary (NYSEARCA:XLY) sector led the inflows, as clients bought stocks in eight sectors, including technology (NYSEARCA:XLF) and financials (NYSEARCA:XLF).

On the other hand, Materials sector (NYSEARCA:XLB) had their first week of outflows in 11 weeks, breaking the longest recent buying streak of the sectors, while communication (NYSEARCA:XLC) and industrials (NYSEARCA:XLI) also saw outflows.

“Clients bought ETFs in six sectors, led by financials and industrials. Energy (NYSEARCA:XLE) ETFs – which have seen the biggest outflows YTD – saw their first inflows in four weeks,” noted Hall in the weekly report.

data.title: Valeura Energy reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Valeura Energy press release (OTCQX:VLERF): Q1 Revenue of $148.08M (-0.9% Y/Y).

  • Oil production of 23,853 bbls/d, an increase of 9% compared to Q1 last year.

  • Adjusted opex trending downward, to US$24.1/bbl, a decrease of 8% compared to Q1 last year.

  • Adjusted Cashflow from Operations of US$74.0 million, an increase of 55% compared to Q1 2024, demonstrating the effects of the corporate restructuring and application of tax loss carry-forwards.

  • The Company's balance sheet remains very strong, with US$239 million cash and no debt; and

  • Adjusted Working Capital of US$254 million.

  • Operations Update and Outlook

    During Q1 2025, Valeura had ongoing production operations at all of its Gulf of Thailand fields, including Jasmine, Manora, Nong Yao, and Wassana fields. Total working interest share production before royalties averaged 23,853 bbls/d, which was in line with management's expectations and consistent with achieving the Company's guidance range for the full year 2025 of 23,000 - 25,500 bbls/d. One drilling rig was under contract throughout the quarter.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: CSCO, CRWV, SBUX and KHC
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Wednesday were slightly higher, following two straight days of strong sentiment. Here are some stocks to watch on Wednesday: 

  • Shares of Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO) will be in focus, ahead of the networking giant's FQ3 2025 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the firm to earn 92 cents per share on revenue of $14.06B. The top-line figure is seen rising 11% Y/Y, as enterprise software continues to recover. Investors will also be looking at Cisco's (NASDAQ:CSCO) artificial intelligence-related offerings. The company on Tuesday unveiled a new partnership with Saudi Arabia's newly formed AI company, Humain AI.
  • CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) stock will garner attention, ahead of the AI hyperscaler's first quarterly results as a public company. Wall Street expects the Livingston, N.J.-based firm to lose 12 cents per share on revenue of $859.77M. According to Macquarie, CoreWeave's AI customizability, along with its highly efficient platform has made it a disruptor in an industry historically dominated by major general-purpose cloud computing platforms.
  • Shares of Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) were -0.2% in pre-market trading. The world's largest coffee chain is facing a fresh round of strikes by baristas in more than 50 stores. The employees have taken issue with Starbucks' (NASDAQ:SBUX) new dress code policy, which requires solid black tops and blue, black, or khaki bottoms. Many baristas argue that the policy imposes an unfair financial burden, as they are only provided with two company shirts and must purchase additional clothing themselves.
  • Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) stock was flat ahead of the opening bell. Reuters reported that the ketchup maker will be spending $3B to upgrade its U.S. factories, citing an interview with Pedro Navio, KHC's president of North America. This would be the company's largest investment in its plants in a decade, even as it struggles with soft sales, low consumer demand and impacts of tariffs. The upgrades will help lower costs by making the plants more efficient, Reuters said.

data.title: Twilio, Microsoft enter multi-year partnership to accelerate conversational AI innovation
data.description:
• Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) announced a multi-year strategic partnership with Microsoft (MSFT) to accelerate adoption of conversational AI solutions for customer engagement.
  • The two companies will collaborate on developing conversational AI customer engagement solutions built on Microsoft Azure AI Foundry and Twilio’s customer engagement platform that combines communications, contextual data, and AI.
  • Key areas include multi-channel AI agents, enhanced support for contact center staff, and multi-modal digital interaction tools.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Schwab daily average revenue trades climb 14% In April, transactional sweep cash dips
data.description:
Charles Schwab's (NYSE:SCHW) daily average revenue trades (DARTs) increased 14% M/M to 8.36M in April, representing a 46% Y/Y increase, the company said on Wednesday. 

Transactional sweep cash fell by $4.5B during the month, ending April at $403.3B, as client allocation decisions partly offset seasonal tax-related outflows, the company said on Wednesday.

Core net new assets of $2.7B fell from $59.1B in March and jumped from $1.0B in April 2024.

Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) stock slipped 0.7% in premarket trading. 

Total client assets of $9.89T were essentially flat M/M and rose 12% Y/Y.

Average margin balances of $77.5B fell 8% M/M and gained 13% Y/Y.

Active brokerage accounts rose 1% M/M and 5% Y/Y to 37.3M.

The company reported 439K of new brokerage accounts, up 13% from March and 22% from April 2024.

Average bank account deposit balances of $84.0B were flat M/M and slipped 5% Y/Y.

data.title: Kraft Heinz investing $3B in U.S. facilities - Reuters
data.description:
To increase efficiency, lower costs, and bring products to market faster, Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) is investing $3B to upgrade its U.S. plants, its largest infrastructure investment in 10 years, Reuters reported Wednesday.

The company operates 30 facilities across the U.S. with nearly all of its products made domestically.

The multi-billion-dollar investment comes on the heels of the company’s decision to lower its FY25 guidance, warning of a “volatile operating environment.” At that time, CEO Carlos Abrams-Rivera indicated that the company was “dedicated to increasing investments and drive product and brand superiority to deliver more value for consumers,” amid an environment impacted by macroeconomic pressures such as tariffs and inflation.

To mitigate the impact on consumers from higher input costs, Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) is considering several strategies to remain competitive, including reformulating some products, and changing suppliers. Last week, Reuters also reported that the company is asking its coffee suppliers for 60-days’ notice before hiking prices.

“In regards to pricing [or the tariffs], we are trying to do everything we possibly can to minimize the amount of price necessary,” Kraft Heinz CFO Andre Maciel said on the company’s Q1 earnings call.

“We are stepping up productivity in the year. We started the year expecting 3.5% of COGS. Now we're expecting a little more than that. So we are taking all the possible levers,” he added on the call.
